headline: Chip rally threat hits highest level since 2015
detail: This is the single most important headline for your portfolio today. You are 90%+ tech/chip names and hold SMH, SOXL, and SPAL as direct sector vehicles. A threat to the chip rally at a 9-year high is not background noise - it is a direct risk to your largest exposure cluster, amplified by leverage and margin.
rec: No new chip buys today. Watch SMH - if it rolls over from current levels, SOXL's $200 stop becomes urgent. Hold existing positions but tighten mental stops; the scanner shows no chip setups with a fresh catalyst to offset this risk.

headline: Fed's Warsh warns: don't expect rate-cut hints
detail: Rate-cut hope has been a key multiple-expansion driver for high-growth tech names like CRWD. Warsh explicitly telling markets not to expect forward guidance kills a near-term catalyst. This keeps valuation pressure on anything pricing in cuts, and CRWD at $777 with a 8% gain is already stretched.
rec: CRWD has a scanner setup (entry 760.78-761.54, stop 756.11) but no fresh catalyst today - actually the Fed headline is a mild headwind. Hold your position, respect $756 as a danger zone, and don't add here without a pullback.

headline: Tech leads first-half gains but biggest winners weren't U.S.
detail: This confirms your tech-heavy book was generally in the right sector, but flags that U.S. tech may have less runway than international names as the second half starts. Rotation risk is real if global tech keeps outperforming - that's a slow-burn headwind for QQQ and SPY concentration.
rec: No action needed today - QQQ and SPY are both in the green and above stops. Just worth knowing that the easy first-half tech trade may be harder from here; don't chase if QQQ softens.

headline: ADP: 98,000 jobs added in June, labor market not great
detail: A weak jobs number is a mixed signal - soft enough to keep rate-cut hopes alive eventually, but too weak to signal strong consumer/business spending. For chip and growth names, it's roughly neutral to slightly positive (keeps Fed from hiking), but Warsh's comments today undercut any rate-cut excitement.
rec: No action - this doesn't change your trade plan for any specific holding. The Fed commentary from Warsh is the more relevant macro input for your book today.
